items.title: Line vs branch coverage on the same function
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  Start["def classify(score)"] --> If{"score > 0.8?"}
  If -- yes --> A["return 'high'"]
  If -- no --> B["return 'low'"]
  T1["Test: classify(0.9)"] -.executes.-> A
  T1 -. never visits .-> B
  Note1["Line coverage: 100% (no untouched lines)"]
  Note2["Branch coverage: 50% (no-branch never taken)"]
items.caption: A single happy-path test can hit every line but leave half the branches untested. Branch coverage is the stricter metric.

items.title: How coverage tooling works
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Test["pytest run"] --> Instr["coverage.py instruments bytecode"]
  Instr --> Run["each executed line/branch recorded"]
  Run --> Data[".coverage data file"]
  Data --> Report["text / HTML / XML report"]
  Report --> CI["CI gate: minimum % + diff coverage"]
  Report --> Eng["engineer reads HTML heatmap"]
items.caption: coverage.py instruments at runtime, persists hit/miss data, then renders the report after the suite finishes.
